growthcleanr 2.99.0
  • Articles
  • Reference
  • Changelog

Exclusion codes

2026-07-18

Source: vignettes/exclusion-codes.Rmd
exclusion-codes.Rmd

Purpose. This file is the canonical catalog of every value cleangrowth() can place in the exclude (Summary) and exclude_detailed (Detailed) output columns.

Code truth. The authoritative enumeration in code is in R/child_clean.R: the two crosswalk vectors exclude.map.summary / exclude.map.detailed and the factor-level vectors exclude.levels.summary / exclude.levels.detailed. The algorithm continues to emit its original internal step literals (exclude.levels.peds + exclude.levels.adult); a mapping layer at the end of output assembly translates those internal codes into the Summary and Detailed codes below. Any Summary/Detailed code that is not a level in the corresponding exclude.levels.* vector becomes a silent NA in the factor output, so this catalog and those vectors must stay identical.

Consolidation rationale. Many internal codes were separated largely for debugging and carry little information for most users (RV/firstRV/allRV passes, -All variants, round iterations, the child-vs-adult algorithm split). The Summary level consolidates these. Most users want an overall summary of why values were excluded; stratifying by age is easy for them, re-consolidating codes is not.

Output levels

growthcleanr reports each value at one of multiple verbosity levels:

  • Summary (default) — the value in the exclude column. Consolidated across steps and across the child/adult algorithms; the smallest useful reason set for the typical user. 14 codes.
  • Detailed (opt-in via exclude_detail = TRUE or full_detail = TRUE) — adds an exclude_detailed column. Step-level; keeps the child (-C-) / adult (-A-) algorithm prefix; folds away the repeated-value (RV / firstRV / allRV), -All, and round-iteration distinctions. Every Detailed code maps to exactly one Summary code.
  • Internal — the per-step literals the algorithm emits today (including RV passes, -All variants, round iteration).

Surfacing. Follows the existing cf_detail / corr_detail / full_detail convention: exclude always carries Summary; exclude_detailed is added only when requested, so both views are available without re-running.

Summary factor order (as set in exclude.levels.summary): Include, then shared codes, then exclusions in approximate algorithm-step order — Include → Exclude-Not-GC-Param, Exclude-Missing-Info, Exclude-HC-Out-of-Range, Exclude-Identical, Exclude-Extraneous → Exclude-Hard-Limit, Exclude-CF, Exclude-Scale-Max, Exclude-Extreme, Exclude-Pattern, Exclude-Pair, Exclude-Single, Exclude-Traj-Uneval.

Shared / “for all” codes (level-independent — identical at every level)

These do not vary by level and carry no -C-/-A- prefix.

Code Algorithm Step When
Include both — Value passes all checks (binary bin_exclude = Include).
Exclude-Not-GC-Param both preprocessing param is present but not one gc handles. New behavior — the row is set aside at entry with its measurement preserved and rebound at the end (see Implementation notes).
Exclude-Missing-Info both preprocessing Missing or unevaluable required field: NA/NaN measurement, measurement < 0 (new), measurement == 0, agedays < 0, NA agedays after the child/adult split, missing param, or unusable sex.
Exclude-HC-Out-of-Range child preprocessing HC with agedays > 5 × 365.25 (WHO HC z-score reference ends at 5 y).
Exclude-Identical both child 13 / adult 9H, 10W Identical to the included value for the same SPA (same-day duplicate, kept-one loser).
Exclude-Extraneous both child 13 / adult 9H, 10W Non-selected, non-identical same-day extraneous value.

Renames from prior internal code: Exclude-Missing → Exclude-Missing-Info; Exclude-Not-Cleaned → Exclude-HC-Out-of-Range.

Temporary (never returned): Exclude-C-Temp-Same-Day is an internal child Step 5 flag and must be resolved before output at every level (it maps to Exclude-Extraneous if it ever reached output).

Child crosswalk (Detailed → Summary)

Detailed child codes keep the -C- prefix. Not param-specific (the param column carries the parameter).

Summary code Child Step(s) Detailed code(s) folded in Reason
Exclude-CF 6 Exclude-C-CF Deemed likely carried forward from the prior value.
Exclude-Hard-Limit 7 Exclude-C-Hard-Limit Population implausible value (outside population hard limits). Internal literal Exclude-C-PIV.
Exclude-Extreme 9, 11 Exclude-C-Evil-Twins (9), Exclude-C-Traj-Extreme (11) Extreme outlier: adjacent extreme value pair/group (Step 9), or extreme trajectory deviation / EWMA1 (Step 11).
Exclude-Pattern 15, 16, 17 Exclude-C-Traj (15/16), Exclude-C-Abs-Diff (17) Moderate trajectory outlier (EWMA2; middle, first, last, birth WT, birth HT/HC, all sub-variants) or height/HC velocity (abs-diff) violation.
Exclude-Pair 19 Exclude-C-Pair Subject with 2 included measurements at Step 19, one excluded.
Exclude-Single 19 Exclude-C-Single Subject with 1 included measurement at Step 19, excluded.
Exclude-Traj-Uneval 21 Exclude-C-Unevaluable-Trajectory Unevaluable trajectory: so many values meet error criteria the subject is deemed unevaluable.

(Shared Exclude-Identical / Exclude-Extraneous from child Step 13 are listed in the shared table above; the detailed level uses the same names, not -C--prefixed.)

Adult crosswalk (Detailed → Summary)

Detailed adult codes keep the -A- prefix. Adult step suffix H/W indicates HT/WT scope. Adult has no Exclude-Pair — the adult 2-distinct-value pair/window checks are trajectory-disagreement and fold into Exclude-Pattern (this avoids colliding with the child “sparse 2-measurement subject” meaning of Exclude-Pair).

Summary code Adult Step(s) Detailed code(s) folded in Reason
Exclude-Hard-Limit 1H/1W Exclude-A-Hard-Limit Population implausible value (outside population hard limits). Internal literal Exclude-A-PIV.
Exclude-Scale-Max 4W Exclude-A-Scale-Max (+ internal Scale-Max-Identical, Scale-Max-RV-Propagated) Weight at the designated scale maximum (all scale-max variants fold here).
Exclude-Extreme 9Wa, 9Wb Exclude-A-Evil-Twins (9Wa), Exclude-A-Traj-Extreme (9Wb) Extreme outlier: adjacent extreme weight pair (9Wa) or extreme EWMA weight outlier (9Wb).
Exclude-Pattern 10Ha, 10Hb, 11Wa, 11Wa2, 11Wb Exclude-A-Ord-Pair (10Ha), Exclude-A-Window (10Hb), Exclude-A-2D-Ordered (11Wa), Exclude-A-2D-Non-Ordered (11Wa2), Exclude-A-Traj-Moderate + Exclude-A-Traj-Moderate-Error-Load (11Wb) Trajectory/pattern disagreement: 2-distinct HT pair outside band, 3+-distinct HT window, 2D ordered/non-ordered WT pair, or moderate EWMA WT outlier (incl. error-load escalation).
Exclude-Single 13 Exclude-A-Single Subject with 1 measurement at Step 13, excluded.
Exclude-Traj-Uneval 14 Exclude-A-Unevaluable-Trajectory Unevaluable trajectory: error ratio exceeds threshold.

Detailed level retains Exclude-A-Traj-Moderate-Error-Load as a distinct code (the -Error-Load escalation inside 11Wb); the -RV copy folds into it. All other RV/firstRV/allRV/-All/-RV-Propagated passes fold into their base Detailed code.

Internal-level codes that fold up: adult firstRV / allRV / -RV-Propagated passes of the Traj-Extreme and Traj-Moderate families, -All variants (Ord-Pair-All, Window-All), the within-trajectory -Error-Load-RV escalation (→ Exclude-Pattern), and Exclude-A-Scale-Max-RV-Propagated (→ Exclude-Scale-Max).

Notes on current behavior

  • Exclude-Not-GC-Param. Rows whose param is not one growthcleanr cleans are set aside at entry with their original measurement preserved, given the internal code Exclude-Not-GC-Param, and rebound at output (rather than being converted to a missing value).
  • measurement < 0 → Exclude-Missing-Info, alongside measurement == 0 and NA/NaN (both child and adult paths). sex = NA resolves to Exclude-Missing-Info as well (via a missing z-score in preprocessing).
  • Population implausible value. Output codes are Summary Exclude-Hard-Limit and Detailed Exclude-C-Hard-Limit / Exclude-A-Hard-Limit. The internal step literals remain Exclude-C-PIV / Exclude-A-PIV (PIV = population implausible value, formerly BIV, “biologically implausible”); the output-mapping layer renames them to Hard-Limit. The name reflects that implausibility here is judged solely against population reference limits — which, especially at tighter adult permissiveness levels, can exclude biologically plausible values — not against the patient’s own trajectory. (Exclude-Single is also population-limit-based, but uses different limits/methods than the PIV step.)
  • Adult Scale-Max: all scale-max variants (Exclude-A-Scale-Max, Exclude-A-Scale-Max-Identical, Exclude-A-Scale-Max-RV-Propagated) fold to Exclude-Scale-Max.

Developed by Carrie Daymont, Robert Grundmeier, Jeffrey Miller, Diego Campos, Dan Chudnov, Hannah De los Santos.

Site built with pkgdown 2.2.1.
